title: Eve + Effect Agent Spike Tasks
principles: Ship one end-to-end Eve agent slice before adding Sendblue, email, Notion, or memory.
Keep Eve filesystem runtime code in apps/agent.
Keep Effect Schema contracts, named operations, errors, and layers in @bundjil/eve-effect.

Use Effect Schema as the source of truth; pass it to Eve through a helper that combines Schema.toStandardJSONSchemaV1 and Schema.toStandardSchemaV1 unless a concrete Eve API requires another edge contract.
Never commit AI Gateway or provider secrets.
globalVerification: GET /eve/v1/info against the local Eve dev server
POST /eve/v1/session against the local Eve dev server
AI Gateway-backed response with a confirmed cheap model, or documented missing-credential blocker

implementationImprovementAuditCounter.description: Before accepting each task, run parent audit passes for ownership/call graph, Effect implementation quality, and verification coverage. Require additional passes if code ownership, Effect flow, Standard JSON Schema usage, or verification evidence is weak.

tasks.title: Install and inspect Eve locally
tasks.description: Add Eve to the workspace in the smallest Bun-compatible way, inspect node_modules/eve/docs, and record the exact tool/channel/schema APIs available to the implementation.
implementationNotes: Use the local .local/references/eve and .local/references/personal-agent-template checkouts plus node_modules/eve/docs after install.
Confirm installed Eve defineTool types still accept StandardJSONSchemaV1 for inputSchema and outputSchema.
Confirm the exact local dev command and HTTP port behavior for apps/agent.
Installed eve@0.20.0 as a root devDependency and added the root script `bun run eve -- ...` so the Bun workspace exposes the Eve CLI before `apps/agent` exists.

Installed docs inspected: node_modules/eve/README.md:40-78 and 119-150; node_modules/eve/docs/tools/overview.mdx:14-35; node_modules/eve/docs/channels/eve.mdx:6-27; node_modules/eve/docs/reference/project-layout.md:6-19 and 45-63; node_modules/eve/docs/agent-config.md:20-24; node_modules/eve/docs/reference/cli.md:87-125.

Installed types inspected: node_modules/eve/dist/src/public/definitions/tool.d.ts:113-145, node_modules/eve/dist/src/shared/tool-definition.d.ts:25-37, node_modules/eve/dist/src/shared/json-schema.d.ts:4-8, and node_modules/eve/dist/src/compiled/@standard-schema/spec/index.d.ts:77-116. `defineTool` accepts StandardJSONSchemaV1 for both `inputSchema` and `outputSchema`, and public tool schemas accept StandardJSONSchemaV1 or plain JsonObject.

Effect v4 conversion APIs inspected in node_modules/.bun/effect@4.0.0-beta.74/node_modules/effect/dist/Schema.d.ts:884-899 and source node_modules/.bun/effect@4.0.0-beta.74/node_modules/effect/src/Schema.ts:1045-1147. `Schema.toStandardJSONSchemaV1(schema)` adds `~standard.jsonSchema`; `Schema.toStandardSchemaV1(...)` adds `~standard.validate` to an existing `~standard`, so the later Eve helper can combine both.

Local references inspected: .local/references/eve@79e9959 packages/eve/src/public/definitions/tool.ts:138-173 and packages/eve/src/shared/tool-definition.ts:33-54; .local/references/personal-agent-template@b521281 package.json:22-50, agent/agent.ts:1-15, agent/channels/eve.ts:1-34, agent/tools/weather.ts:58-130, and scripts/ensure-eve-dev.mjs:1-31. The template is on older `eve` ^0.13.8 and uses Zod examples, so installed eve@0.20.0 docs/types are authoritative for Bundjil.

Local dev behavior confirmed with installed Eve: `eve dev --help` reports `--port` defaults to `$PORT`, then 2000; node_modules/eve/dist/src/internal/nitro/host/ports.d.ts:5-8 defines DEFAULT_DEVELOPMENT_SERVER_PORT = 2000; a temporary smoke app run with `/Users/cooper/Projects/bundjil/node_modules/.bin/eve dev --no-ui` listened at http://127.0.0.1:2000/ and GET /eve/v1/health returned `{ "ok": true, "status": "ready", "workflowId": "workflow//eve//workflowEntry" }`. node_modules/eve/docs/reference/cli.md:96-100 still says 3000 and is treated as stale for eve@0.20.0.
mandatoryVerification: Record inspected Eve docs/types in the implementation notes

evidence: Pass 1 - ownership/call graph: Kept the change to root package/lock plus evidence docs. Did not create `apps/agent` or `packages/eve-effect`; later tasks still own those boundaries.

Pass 2 - Effect/Eve schema boundary: Confirmed installed `defineTool` overloads and public tool schema contracts accept StandardJSONSchemaV1, and confirmed Effect v4 exposes both Standard Schema and Standard JSON Schema adapters needed for the later helper.

Pass 3 - verification coverage: Ran dependency hygiene after install, live-probed a temporary Eve app on port 2000, and passed `bun install --frozen-lockfile`, `bun run check-types`, and `bun run verification`.
Parent audit 1 - ownership/call graph: Reviewed commit b85584e and confirmed only root Eve install/CLI access plus docs/task evidence changed; app and package creation remain deferred to later tasks.

Parent audit 2 - implementation quality: Confirmed the root `eve` script keeps the research dependency visible to dependency hygiene, the task records installed `StandardJSONSchemaV1` behavior, and no zod mirror or premature framework package was introduced.

Parent audit 3 - verification coverage: Parent reran `bun install --frozen-lockfile`, `bun run check-types`, `bun run verification`, `git diff --check HEAD~1 HEAD`, and a temp-artifact scan before accepting the task.

tasks.title: Create @bundjil/eve-effect contracts and named operations
tasks.description: Create packages/eve-effect with Effect Schema contracts, tagged errors, Context.Service or service tag, live/memory layers, and the first named operation getWorkspaceStatus.
implementationNotes: Created the compiled Bun workspace package `@bundjil/eve-effect` under `packages/eve-effect` with package exports, tsconfig, vitest config, README, source, and tests following the existing `@bundjil/core` and `@bundjil/effect-start` package shape.

Added canonical Effect Schema contracts `WorkspaceStatusInput` and `WorkspaceStatusSuccess`, schema-derived tool type aliases, tagged error classes for `BundjilAgentOperationError`, `BundjilAgentSchemaError`, and `BundjilAgentGatewayConfigError`, and the `WorkspaceStatusFailure` tagged-union schema.

Added `BundjilAgentOperations` as a `Context.Service` boundary with `BundjilAgentOperationsLive`, `BundjilAgentOperationsMemory`, and the named `getWorkspaceStatus` operation. The live implementation delegates deterministic workspace data to `@bundjil/core` and encodes output through `Schema.encodeEffect` with schema errors mapped to `BundjilAgentSchemaError`.

Added the Eve boundary helper `toEveSchema(schema)` in `src/eve/tool-adapter.ts`, combining Effect's `Schema.toStandardSchemaV1` and `Schema.toStandardJSONSchemaV1` adapters and naming the public Standard Schema contract through direct `@standard-schema/spec` types.
No `apps/agent` files, zod schemas, provider secrets, local DTO mirrors, unsafe casts, or app-owned Eve runtime files were introduced in this task.
mandatoryVerification: Flat meaningful Effect.gen programs for primary operations
Typed errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError where relevant
Canonical Effect Schema contracts and schema-derived types from @bundjil/eve-effect
No unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, or trivial wrappers/helpers
completionCriteria: WorkspaceStatusInput, WorkspaceStatusSuccess, and tagged error schemas exist
BundjilAgentOperations.getWorkspaceStatus is testable without Eve or model access
Layer.memory or equivalent mock layer exists for tests

evidence: Pass 1 - ownership/call graph: Kept Task 2 scoped to `packages/eve-effect`, `bun.lock`, and this task evidence. The named operation delegates to `@bundjil/core`; no Eve app boundary, channels, model config, Sendblue, Notion, or provider-secret code was added.

Pass 2 - Effect implementation quality: Primary operations use flat `Effect.gen`, `Context.Service`, `Layer.effect`, `Layer.succeed`, `Effect.fn`, `Effect.withSpan`, Effect Schema contracts, schema-backed tagged errors, and `Effect.mapError` for schema failures. The Eve adapter uses Effect's Standard Schema and Standard JSON Schema primitives instead of zod or manual DTO adapters.
Pass 3 - verification coverage: Passed `bun run --filter @bundjil/eve-effect test`, `bun run check-types`, and full `bun run verification` after formatter, knip, typecheck, and repo test gates.

Parent audit 1 - ownership/call graph: Reviewed commit 2f764df and confirmed the new package owns Effect/Eve contracts while `apps/agent` remains untouched; parent updated `@bundjil/core` package summary so the live operation reports the newly introduced `@bundjil/eve-effect` package.

Parent audit 2 - implementation quality: Confirmed `BundjilAgentOperations` is a named `Context.Service`, live and memory layers are testable without Eve/model access, `toEveSchema` is the only real-boundary helper, and scans found no zod import, unsafe cast, DTO mirror, or stringly branch in the new package.

Parent audit 3 - verification coverage: Parent ran `bun run --filter @bundjil/eve-effect test`, `bun --filter @bundjil/eve-effect build`, `bun run check-types`, `bun run verification`, and `git diff --check HEAD~1 HEAD` before accepting the task.

tasks.title: Create apps/agent with a minimal Eve agent
tasks.description: Create the Eve filesystem app with instructions, model config, and a workspace_status tool that delegates into @bundjil/eve-effect.
implementationNotes: Created the Bun workspace app package `@bundjil/agent` under `apps/agent` with Eve runtime scripts (`dev`, `dev:no-ui`, `build`), TypeScript checking, and Vitest tests.

Added `agent/instructions.md` with concise operational instructions for the minimal Bundjil personal agent and explicit non-claims for Sendblue, Cloudflare email, Notion, Vercel Connect, memory, and live channel surfaces.

Added `agent/agent.ts` using Eve `defineAgent` without an authored `name` field. The model is environment-overridable through `process.env["BUNDJIL_AGENT_MODEL"]` with the confirmed Vercel AI Gateway default `google/gemini-2.5-flash`; no provider secret or gateway key is committed.

Added `agent/tools/workspace_status.ts` as the Eve filename-slug tool. It uses `defineTool` from `eve/tools`, `toEveSchema(WorkspaceStatusInput)`, `toEveSchema(WorkspaceStatusSuccess)`, and delegates execution to `getWorkspaceStatus(input).pipe(Effect.provide(BundjilAgentOperationsLive))`.

The current `@bundjil/eve-effect` package exports canonical schemas and operations from the package root and `toEveSchema` from `@bundjil/eve-effect/eve/tool-adapter`; the app imports from those existing canonical exports instead of changing the already-completed Task 2 package surface.

Added `test/workspace-status-tool.test.ts` proving the Eve tool executor runs without Eve server or model access, returns deterministic workspace status including `@bundjil/eve-effect`, and wires the exported input/output schemas to Effect Standard Schema validation plus Standard JSON Schema metadata.

Added an app-local `.gitignore` for Eve/Nitro/build artifacts and an app-local Oxlint tools-directory exception so Eve's required underscore tool slug `workspace_status.ts` can coexist with the repo filename-case rule.
Updated `knip.json` so dependency hygiene treats Eve filesystem app files (`agent/agent.ts`, `agent/tools/**/*.ts`, and tests) as workspace entrypoints.
mandatoryVerification: Eve tool execute path delegates to the Effect named operation
Tool edge validates input and output using Effect Schema via a toEveSchema helper that combines Schema.toStandardJSONSchemaV1 and Schema.toStandardSchemaV1
No zod mirror schemas are introduced unless a concrete Eve API requires them
completionCriteria: apps/agent/agent/agent.ts uses an environment-overridable cheap model string

evidence: Pass 1 - ownership/call graph: Kept Eve filesystem runtime, model config, tool file, app tests, and app artifact ignores in `apps/agent`; the tool delegates to the existing `@bundjil/eve-effect` named operation and live layer, which delegates deterministic workspace data to `@bundjil/core`. No Task 4 HTTP/Gateway proof, Sendblue, Cloudflare email, Notion, Vercel Connect, memory, or UI surface was added.

Pass 2 - implementation quality: The app reuses canonical `WorkspaceStatusInput`, `WorkspaceStatusSuccess`, `BundjilAgentOperationsLive`, `getWorkspaceStatus`, and `toEveSchema`; tool execution is a small inline Effect boundary via `Effect.runPromise`, no zod mirror schemas, unsafe casts, DTO copies, switch branches, or provider-secret config were introduced, and the only lint exception is app-local for Eve's required underscore filename slug.

Pass 3 - verification coverage: Passed `bun install`, `bun install --frozen-lockfile`, `bun run --filter @bundjil/agent test`, `bun run check-types`, `bun run check`, `bun run knip`, `bun run --filter @bundjil/agent build`, stale-pattern scans for zod and provider secrets under `apps/agent`, and full `bun run verification`. Live `/eve/v1/info`, `/eve/v1/session`, and AI Gateway proof remain intentionally deferred to Task 4.

Parent audit 1 - ownership/call graph: Reviewed commit 3240ee7 and confirmed `apps/agent` owns Eve filesystem runtime, model config, and tool wiring while `@bundjil/eve-effect` remains the shared Effect operation boundary.

Parent audit 2 - implementation quality: Confirmed `defineAgent` has no authored name, the `workspace_status` Eve tool reuses canonical schemas and `toEveSchema`, execution delegates through `getWorkspaceStatus(...).pipe(Effect.provide(BundjilAgentOperationsLive))`, and scans found no zod import or committed provider secret.

Parent audit 3 - verification coverage: Parent ran `bun run --filter @bundjil/agent test`, `bun run --filter @bundjil/agent build`, `bun run check-types`, `bun run verification`, `git diff --check HEAD~1 HEAD`, confirmed the default `google/gemini-2.5-flash` model string on the official Vercel AI Gateway model page, then added explicit Node 24 setup to GitHub CI/Release after the remote Eve build proved the runner's default Node 22 was incompatible.

tasks.title: Verify the local Eve agent through HTTP and AI Gateway
tasks.description: Run the Eve dev server, prove /eve/v1/info works, then POST a prompt to /eve/v1/session and capture a response that uses the workspace_status tool.
mandatoryVerification: GET /eve/v1/info returns the configured agent metadata
POST /eve/v1/session starts a session and returns usable Eve session details
At least one prompt receives a model response through AI Gateway with a confirmed cheap model
If AI Gateway credentials are missing, record the exact missing env var and stop before faking success
completionCriteria: Local verification commands and outputs are recorded in the active evidence
No secret values are printed or committed
The agent response proves the tool path can run

tasks.title: Write comprehensive Eve + Effect documentation
tasks.description: Document the final app/package shape, local commands, schema boundary, AI Gateway setup, and verification proof.
completionCriteria: Docs explain the production and test call graphs
Docs distinguish committed source from local-only references and secrets
Docs include exact local verification commands

tasks.title: Run final verification and prepare handoff
tasks.description: Run the full repository verification and summarize the implementation evidence.
